audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for no condemnation, Spirit of life, mind of flesh/Spirit, adoption, creation groaning, Spirit intercession, conformed to Son, inseparable love.

verses.restored: There is therefore now no condemnation for those in Messiah Jesus.
For the law/principle of the Spirit of life in Messiah Jesus released you from the law/principle of sin/misalignment and death.

For the impossible thing of the Law, in which it was weak through flesh, the Divine did: sending his own Son in likeness of flesh of sin/misalignment and concerning sin/misalignment, he condemned sin/misalignment in the flesh,
so that the righteous/just requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to flesh but according to Spirit.
For those being according to flesh set their mind on the things of flesh; but those according to Spirit, the things of Spirit.
For the mind of flesh is death, but the mind of Spirit is life and peace.
Because the mind of flesh is hostility toward the Divine, for it does not submit to the Law of the Divine, for it is not able.
Those being in flesh are not able to please the Divine.
But you are not in flesh but in Spirit, if indeed Spirit of the Divine dwells in you. If anyone does not have Spirit of Messiah, this one is not his.
But if Messiah is in you, the body indeed is dead because of sin/misalignment, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness/justice.
If the Spirit of the One who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, the One who raised Messiah from the dead will also make alive your mortal bodies through his Spirit dwelling in you.
So then, siblings, we are debtors, not to flesh, to live according to flesh.
For if you live according to flesh, you are about to die; but if by Spirit you put to death the practices of the body, you will live.
For as many as are led by Spirit of the Divine, these are sons/children of the Divine.
For you did not receive spirit of slavery again into fear, but you received Spirit of adoption, in whom we cry, Abba, Father.
The Spirit itself bears witness together with our spirit that we are children of the Divine.
And if children, also heirs: heirs of the Divine and co-heirs with Messiah, if indeed we suffer together so that we may also be glorified together.
For I calculate that the sufferings of the present time are not worthy compared with the glory about to be unveiled into us.
For the eager expectation of creation awaits the unveiling of the sons/children of the Divine.
For creation was subjected to futility, not willingly, but because of the One who subjected it, upon hope,
that creation itself also will be released from the slavery of decay into the freedom of the glory of the children of the Divine.
For we know that all creation groans together and suffers birth pains together until now.
And not only this, but we ourselves, having the firstfruit of the Spirit, also groan within ourselves, awaiting adoption, the redemption/release of our body.
For in hope we were saved/healed; but hope being seen is not hope. For who hopes for what one sees?
But if we hope for what we do not see, through endurance we await it.
Likewise also the Spirit helps together with our weakness; for what we should pray as is necessary, we do not know, but the Spirit itself intercedes with unspeakable groanings.
The One searching hearts knows what is the mind of the Spirit, because according to the Divine, it intercedes for holy ones.
We know that for those loving the Divine, all things work together into good, for those called according to purpose.
Because those whom he foreknew, he also marked out beforehand to be conformed to the image of his Son, so that he might be firstborn among many siblings.
Those whom he marked out beforehand, these he also called; and those whom he called, these he also made righteous/just; and those whom he made righteous/just, these he also glorified.
What then shall we say to these things? If the Divine is for us, who is against us?
He who did not spare his own Son but handed him over for us all, how will he not also with him graciously give us all things?
Who will bring accusation against the chosen ones of the Divine? The Divine is the one making righteous/just.
Who is the one condemning? Messiah Jesus is the one who died, rather was raised, who is also at the right hand of the Divine, who also intercedes for us.
Who will separate us from the love of Messiah? Pressure, distress, persecution, famine, nakedness, danger, or sword?
Just as it is written: Because of you we are being put to death the whole day; we were counted as sheep of slaughter.
But in all these things we more than conquer through the One who loved us.
For I am persuaded that neither death nor life, neither messengers nor rulers, neither present things nor coming things, nor powers,
neither height nor depth, nor any other creature, will be able to separate us from the love of the Divine in Messiah Jesus our Lord.

translation_notes: Flesh in Romans 8 refers to a domination-pattern opposed to Spirit, not the body as evil. Verse 11 explicitly includes mortal bodies being made alive.
Creation groaning is cosmic liberation language and should be kept ecological/embodied, not reduced to private spirituality.
